reasoning: The ECB deposit facility rate has been flat at 2.000% since June 2025, down from 3.25% in Nov 2024. Recent comprehensive reporting (Reuters, Nov 19 2025; Morningstar, Nov 21 2025) establishes that the ECB is expected to hold rates through at least the end of 2026, with the next meeting on December 18. There are no anticipated rate *hikes*; any minor surprise would likely be a cut. Moreover, there is no policy meeting before the first resolution date (Nov 30), meaning the rate will mechanically remain at 2.000%. For subsequent dates (Dec 23 and Feb 21), inflation and growth forecasts do not support an unexpected tightening cycle, making an increase virtually impossible.
